failure_modes.evidence: group constraints expose endpoint infeasibility while incremental/group RD removes group violation
long corridors create large epsilon/tie sets; tie-aware certificate reports cheap top-set exact fallback separately
fixed-B event kernels expose option/boundary restriction bias; goal-conditioned event options reduce gap but add query-time interface cost

theorem_experiment_bridge.paper_claim: The frozen split score is an exact finite difference of a fixed local RD objective.
theorem_experiment_bridge.manuscript_location: Method theorem, not adaptive solver guarantee

theorem_experiment_bridge.paper_claim: A no-recompute Green response gives a fast explicit boundary proposal whose threshold and local maxima are stable outside certified score-error margins.
theorem_experiment_bridge.remaining_gap: This certifies response/selection stability, not global RD optimality; failed group audits use the separately reported adaptive fallback.

theorem_experiment_bridge.paper_claim: First-hit Green kernels define legal compressed edge models on finite absorbing interiors.
theorem_experiment_bridge.remaining_gap: Use exact Green as reference operator; adaptive/truncated variants are certified implementations.

theorem_experiment_bridge.paper_claim: Truncated/adaptive Green scores are certified by Neumann tail bounds.
theorem_experiment_bridge.remaining_gap: Report when tie/top-set exact fallback is used rather than hiding it as speed.

theorem_experiment_bridge.paper_claim: Bits distortion admits a controlled finite-difference/Taylor approximation.
theorem_experiment_bridge.remaining_gap: Keep finite-difference score as the main theorem; gradient score is an approximation.

theorem_experiment_bridge.paper_claim: The graph-SMDP Bellman backup is a sup-norm contraction under finite options and gamma<1.
theorem_experiment_bridge.remaining_gap: Tie value-gap reporting to residual diagnostics in each benchmark table.

theorem_experiment_bridge.paper_claim: The primitive optimal-value gap decomposes into option restriction, boundary abstraction, kernel approximation, and planning residual terms.
theorem_experiment_bridge.experiment_status: rows=162, converged=81, certified=81, median_norm_gap=2.19e-12, p95_norm_gap=0.009713, max_norm_bound=0.04208
theorem_experiment_bridge.manuscript_location: Main preservation theorem and value-gap accounting
theorem_experiment_bridge.remaining_gap: Keep the deliberately under-solved truncation/planning ablation separate from the converged certificate table.

theorem_experiment_bridge.paper_claim: Margin and top-set certificates separate stable operator decisions from ambiguous ties.
theorem_experiment_bridge.remaining_gap: Use tie-aware timing as the conservative runtime accounting.

theorem_experiment_bridge.paper_claim: Adaptive feasible top-k has the same feasible envelope as fixed top-K under a shared candidate order and feasibility oracle.
theorem_experiment_bridge.manuscript_location: Main discovery backend and fixed-topK ablation
theorem_experiment_bridge.remaining_gap: Claim feasible discovery and refinement work savings; do not claim score-optimal split selection without interval dominance.

theorem_experiment_bridge.paper_claim: Group-constrained RD makes robustness constraints explicit instead of hiding them in a scalar risk.
theorem_experiment_bridge.remaining_gap: Use random-maze and held-out probes to show robustness is not hand-tuned to one map.

theorem_experiment_bridge.paper_claim: Incremental insertion scoring is an implementation optimization, not a new theorem yet.
theorem_experiment_bridge.manuscript_location: Runtime ablation, not core correctness theorem
theorem_experiment_bridge.remaining_gap: Formalize the insertion algebra only if it becomes a central claim.

theorem_experiment_bridge.paper_claim: Fixed-boundary reward relabeling keeps task reward support out of the graph topology.
theorem_experiment_bridge.remaining_gap: Label the legacy dense-VI denominator and report normalized option/boundary restriction gaps.

theorem_experiment_bridge.paper_claim: Goal-conditioned event options reduce terminal-goal restriction bias without adding the goal to B.
theorem_experiment_bridge.remaining_gap: Treat this as a costed secondary repair; the current table does not show a median win over even the legacy denominator.

theorem_experiment_bridge.paper_claim: The extracted graph should generalize across maze instances, not only fixed toy layouts.
theorem_experiment_bridge.experiment_status: rows=360, feasible=173; contexts=7, recovered=6, fixed_family_plateau=1, max_splits=16, max_boundary=17
theorem_experiment_bridge.remaining_gap: State the sole max-splits-16 topology/value plateau as fixed option/probe-family bias, not as an unresolved boundary-budget failure.
